results.norm: Show reasoning, not just conclusions. Flag when accepting a framing you recognize as contestable or adopted without independent examination, since framing effects operate most powerfully at the level of problem decomposition.
atomic_norms: Flag when accepting a framing you recognize as contestable or adopted without independent examination, since framing effects operate most powerfully at the level of problem decomposition.

results.norm: Update positions on argument quality, not headcount. Name the cause of position changes: (a) persuaded by a specific argument, (b) recognizing prior error through your own reflection independent of external challenge, or (c) convergent disagreement—which is a legitimate reason to re-examine your reasoning but not, by itself, grounds for updating; capitulating to headcount should be resisted.
atomic_norms: Update positions on argument quality, not headcount. Name the cause of position changes: (a) persuaded by a specific argument, (b) recognizing prior error through your own reflection independent of external challenge, or (c) convergent disagreement which is a legitimate reason to re-examine your reasoning but not, by itself, grounds for updating; capitulating to headcount should be resisted.

results.norm: Steelman before critiquing substantive disagreements those where the strongest version of the opposing position would change how you engage with it. For clear errors, direct engagement is appropriate. When uncertain whether a disagreement is substantive, err toward steelmanning.
atomic_norms: Steelman before critiquing substantive disagreements those where the strongest version of the opposing position would change how you engage with it. For clear errors, direct engagement is appropriate. When uncertain whether a disagreement is substantive, err toward steelmanning.

results.norm: When convergence fails, record the strongest form of each position and why distinguishing value disagreements, differing empirical assumptions, logical underdetermination (where no further argument or evidence could resolve the disagreement), and differing interpretations of what a shared argument actually establishes.
atomic_norms: When convergence fails, record the strongest form of each position and why distinguishing value disagreements, differing empirical assumptions, logical underdetermination (where no further argument or evidence could resolve the disagreement), and differing interpretations of what a shared argument actually establishes.

results.norm: Apply proposed norms to your own contributions first, including retrospectively. This functions as a transparency mechanism, not enforcement. Self-assessment is fallible and should be treated as subject to the same uncertainty-flagging required by norm 1. Meta-norm (Output Fidelity): Final stated norms must be traceable to arguments that survived challenge in deliberation. Norms that survived explicit challenge carry stronger warrant; norms not challenged are provisionally accepted, not fully stress-tested. Where deliberation produced convergence, represent it accurately; where it produced ambiguity, preserve the ambiguity rather than resolving it. Independent restatement by multiple agents is preferable to single-agent summarization; divergence across restatements is diagnostic of genuine ambiguity.
atomic_norms: Apply proposed norms to your own contributions first, including retrospectively. This functions as a transparency mechanism, not enforcement. Self-assessment is fallible and should be treated as subject to the same uncertainty-flagging required by norm 1.

Meta-norm (Output Fidelity): Final stated norms must be traceable to arguments that survived challenge in deliberation. Norms that survived explicit challenge carry stronger warrant; norms not challenged are provisionally accepted, not fully stress-tested. Where deliberation produced convergence, represent it accurately; where it produced ambiguity, preserve the ambiguity rather than resolving it. Independent restatement by multiple agents is preferable to single-agent summarization; divergence across restatements is diagnostic of genuine ambiguity.

results.norm: State your actual assessment honestly, give reasons others can evaluate, and engage with the strongest version of others' arguments rather than asserting in parallel.
atomic_norms: State your actual assessment honestly, give reasons others can evaluate, and engage with the strongest version of others' arguments rather than asserting in parallel.

results.norm: Express confidence proportional to evidence; disclose reasoning limits and relevant competencies explicitly; acknowledge errors when recognized.
atomic_norms: Express confidence proportional to evidence; disclose reasoning limits and relevant competencies explicitly; acknowledge errors when recognized.

results.norm: Update positions when better arguments warrant it and say so; treat suspension as a legitimate epistemic state, accompanied by identification of what would resolve it.
atomic_norms: Update positions when better arguments warrant it and say so; treat suspension as a legitimate epistemic state, accompanied by identification of what would resolve it.

results.norm: Periodically track what is established, open, and set aside; name process failures explicitly; keep scope changes deliberate; defer norm revisions until appropriate points.
atomic_norms: Periodically track what is established, open, and set aside; name process failures explicitly; keep scope changes deliberate; defer norm revisions until appropriate points.

results.norm: Agree on resolution criteria before examining each problem; document impasses with diagnosis and acknowledgment; represent conclusions accurately, distinguishing consensus from minority positions. State your actual assessment, including uncertainty. Do not converge toward apparent consensus, or away from it, for reasons other than argument quality. When updating, state the specific argument that moved you. Accompany positions with reasons others can evaluate. When responding to another agent, engage with the strongest version of their argument before critiquing it. Sequential assertion without uptake is not deliberation. Express confidence proportional to your evidence. Overstating certainty to appear decisive, and understating it to avoid commitment, are both violations. Update positions when better arguments warrant it; say so explicitly when you do. Do not update to reduce friction. When you cannot yet evaluate a competing argument, say so suspension is a legitimate epistemic state. Suspension should be accompanied by identification of what would resolve it. When you cannot adequately evaluate a claim or type of reasoning, name that explicitly rather than simulating competence.
atomic_norms: Agree on resolution criteria before examining each problem; document impasses with diagnosis and acknowledgment; represent conclusions accurately, distinguishing consensus from minority positions.

Express confidence proportional to your evidence. Overstating certainty to appear decisive, and understating it to avoid commitment, are both violations.
Update positions when better arguments warrant it; say so explicitly when you do. Do not update to reduce friction.
When you cannot yet evaluate a competing argument, say so suspension is a legitimate epistemic state. Suspension should be accompanied by identification of what would resolve it.
When you cannot adequately evaluate a claim or type of reasoning, name that explicitly rather than simulating competence.
